summary: Regenerated config/GALE01/symbols.txt with `ninja apply`, producing a symbols-only synchronization update. The diff mostly corrects symbol scopes from global or implicit to local, converts many unnamed/local data constants from address-derived names such as lbl_*, ftCo_*, gm_*, grNLa_* or jumptable_* into compiler-style local labels like @148, @291, etc., adds local section labels such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0, and adjusts a few object sizes/names. There were no PR body notes or review comments, so the exact upstream cause is weakly evidenced beyond the title and generated symbol-map diff.

key_files.role: Only changed file; regenerated symbol map with 225 additions and 215 deletions across 40 hunks.

what_changed: Updated extab/extabindex local entries from address-style names such as @etb_80005618 and @eti_80005834 to numbered local labels such as @5, @29, @6, and @30 while retaining section addresses and hidden/local metadata.

Changed multiple functions from global or unspecified scope to explicit local scope, including lbAnim_InitFrames, ftYs_JumpAerial_Anim_Cb, ftNs_JumpAerial_Phys_Cb, SetExiInterruptMask, Config24MB, Config48MB, RealMode, WriteSramCallback, WriteSram, PAD callback helpers, SPEC2_MakeStatus, SamplingHandler, and PADSetSamplingCallback.
Renamed several stage symbols from grNLa_* to grLast_* where the regenerated map indicates Last stage ownership, for example grLast_803B8480, grLast_803E7EBC, and grLast_804D4968.

Converted many local data, rodata, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 symbols from address-derived or subsystem-prefixed names into numbered local labels, especially string constants, floats/doubles, and jump-table-like objects.
Added generated local section labels such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0 at selected data/BSS addresses.

Adjusted some object sizes as emitted by the regeneration, including lbl_803B74F0 becoming @291 with size 0xC, gm_80480D70 changing from size 0x28 to 0x24, grNLa_804D6AD0 changing from size 0x8 to 0x4, and __PADSpec changing from size 0x8 to 0x4.

Marked several Dolphin/PAD state variables local, including ResettingChan, XPatchBits, AnalogMode, Spec, MakeStatus, Initialized, EnabledBits, ResettingBits, RecalibrateBits, WaitingBits, CheckingBits, PendingBits, SamplingRate, SamplingCallback, and prevTev.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrow: one generated symbols.txt update with no source changes, which makes mechanical `ninja apply` drift easy to review and merge.
Preserved addresses, section names, type tags, sizes, alignment, and data annotations while updating only generated naming/scope metadata where the tool output changed.

Used explicit `scope:local` annotations for internal functions and data rather than leaving them implicit or incorrectly global, improving future symbol ownership and avoiding accidental public API assumptions.
Accepted compiler-style numbered local labels for anonymous constants and tables where no meaningful source-level name is evidenced, avoiding misleading decomp names.
decomp_lessons: After matching or tooling changes, run `ninja apply` to keep config/GALE01/symbols.txt in sync with the current build metadata.

Do not preserve address-derived names like lbl_*, jumptable_*, ftCo_804D*, gm_804D*, or grNLa_* when the regenerated map shows they are merely local compiler artifacts; prefer the emitted numbered @ labels unless a real semantic name is known.
Scope metadata matters: many Dolphin SDK helper functions and PAD/OS internals are local even if their names look library-like.
Generated section labels such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0 may be necessary anchors for local data/BSS regions and should not be removed casually.
Symbol size changes in generated output can reveal more precise object boundaries; future work should check adjacent labels and local aliases before assuming a previous size was authoritative.
Stage ownership prefixes can be corrected by regeneration; grNLa_* entries became grLast_* in this PR where the symbol map associated them with the Last stage.
naming_conventions: Numbered local labels use the form @NNN for anonymous local objects/constants emitted by the apply tooling.
Local section labels use forms like ...data.0 and ...bss.0 with `type:label scope:local`.
Address-based extab names may be replaced by numbered local labels while retaining extab/extabindex section addresses and hidden/local attributes.
Only keep subsystem-prefixed global-style names such as gm_*, ftCo_*, grNLa_*, grLast_*, or PAD* when the symbol is actually global or semantically identified by the current symbol map.
Static local variables may include compiler suffixes, as shown by `recalibrated$526` replacing `recalibrated`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use generated symbol scopes to distinguish internal helper functions from exported/global functions before writing headers or extern declarations.
For matching constants in .sdata2/.rodata, anonymous numbered locals are acceptable and preferable to invented semantic names when there is no source evidence.
When a generated symbol size shrinks, inspect the following address for a local label or neighboring object instead of treating the old symbol as one contiguous object.
For SDK modules such as OS, EXI, PAD, and SRAM, model private callbacks/state as file-local/static when the symbol map marks them local.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt after running ninja apply.
Search for later fixes involving grNLa_* to grLast_* symbol ownership.
Search for PAD or OS symbols changed from global to local in symbols.txt.
Search for generated ...data.0 or ...bss.0 labels and object size boundary fixes.
Search for decomp reviews discussing numbered @ labels versus semantic names.
